styleHead: STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation
castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. A long stretch. THE NARRATOR listens, head tilted. The muffled beat. slows. falters. Silence. He heaves the bed aside. Kneels. Places his hand upon the old man's heart. Holds it there. Many minutes. No pulsation. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> says "At length it ceased. The old man was dead.". Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> still wears loose dark nightshirt, barefoot, holding lantern Camera directive: Close-up of Narrator’s face as the beat ceases, quiet realization: the old man is dead. Performance: Acting intensity 5/10: Quiet finality eyes, tension release, soft-exhale lips, peaceful confirm Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O. Match appearance of reference <IMAGE_1> exactly.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "At length it ceased. The old man was dead.". Start speaking immediately with "At" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient bed: muffled heartbeat. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CONTEXT (prior clip in scene — new cast plate refs attached; match location/lighting if still valid; identity from CHARACTER VARIABLES + locked plates only):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. A long stretch. THE NARRATOR listens, head tilted. The muffled beat. slows. falters. Silence. He heaves the bed aside. Kneels. Places his hand upon the old man's heart. Holds it there. Many minutes. No pulsation. Character_The_Narrator still wears loose dark nightshirt, barefoot, holding lantern Camera directive: Medium tracking Narrator listening head-tilted, then heaving bed aside, kneeling to place hand on heart. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Tilted intense listening, eyes narrow then widen at silence, solemn Optics: f/2.8 moderate depth of field, long observational hold Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray
